title: Preload IPC surface has no regression coverage
reasoning: The preload file is the renderer-facing API contract for every repository, finding, feature, triage, command, stream, and git operation, but the feature declares no linked tests and the package test command is available. A wrong IPC channel, malformed payload object, or broken stream unsubscribe would not be caught before release.
recommendation: Add focused Vitest coverage that mocks electron's contextBridge and ipcRenderer, imports the preload module, and asserts each exposed method invokes the expected channel with the expected payload. Include a stream test proving onStream registers a handler, forwards only the payload to the listener, and removes the exact handler returned by the unsubscribe function.
whyTestsDoNotAlreadyCoverThis: The feature metadata lists no tests for src/preload/index.ts, and no linked test evidence was provided for the exposed API contract.
suggestedRegressionTest: Create a preload test that mocks electron, captures the object passed to contextBridge.exposeInMainWorld('clawpatch', api), then exercises repo.add, findings.get, triage.set, commands.run, git.diff, and commands.onStream against mocked ipcRenderer.invoke/on/removeListener.
minimumFixScope: Add a test file for src/preload/index.ts and any minimal Vitest mock setup needed for the electron module.
